summary: Tested Price Estimator feature (new widget on landing page). All 10 backend API tests pass. All 10 frontend UI/integration tests pass. Feature works end-to-end: default Delhi/Wedding/200 shows ₹4.0L-₹25.0L with 11 venues, all selector changes update live, Browse CTA navigates with correct query params, No venues state handled gracefully, all 12 landing page sections still render correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Mumbai (and other non-Delhi/Gurgaon cities) return venue_count=0 because no approved venues are seeded for those cities — this is a data limitation, not a code bug. The 'No venues found' fallback handles it gracefully.

The 401 console errors on the landing page are from /api/rms/top-performers endpoint for unauthenticated users — these are expected and non-breaking (section falls back to 'Loading venue experts...').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Price Estimator feature fully verified in iteration_61. All backend + frontend tests pass. Key data points: Delhi/Wedding/200 = 11 venues, ₹4.0L-₹25.0L, avg ₹13.6L. Gurgaon/Corporate/200 = 6 venues, ₹5.0L-₹15.0L. Mumbai has 0 venues. Section order: HOW IT WORKS (y=1542) → PRICE ESTIMATOR (y=1963) → FEATURED VENUES (y=2646). The previous iteration_60 outstanding issue (VenuePublicPage.js old gold #C8A960) was NOT retested - still needs to be addressed.

backend_tests: Response includes all required fields: min_price, max_price, avg_price, venue_count, guests, city, event_type ✓
frontend_tests: Price estimator section exists with data-testid='price-estimator-section' ✓
Section is below HOW IT WORKS: y=1963 > y=1542 ✓
Changing city to Mumbai → 'No venues found for this combination. Try different filters.' ✓
Changing event type to Corporate → updates results ✓
Changing guests to 500 → updates results with new prices ✓
All 12 landing page sections (hero, why-us, social-proof, how-it-works, price-estimator, featured-venues, city-coverage, top-performers, work-with-us, testimonials, final-cta, footer) visible ✓
